author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
hā hā mora gaura⋅kiśora
kabe dayā kari’, śrī-godruma-vane,
dekhā dibe manaḥcora

ānanda⋅sukhada⋅, kuñjera bhitare,
gadādhare bāme kari’
kāñcana⋅varaṇa, cā̐cara cikura,
naṭana suveśa dhari’

dekhite dekhite, śrī⋅rādhā⋅mādhava,
rūpete karibe ālā
sakhīgaṇa⋅saṅge, karibe naṭana,
galete mohana-mālā

anaṅga⋅mañjarī, sadaya hoiyā,
e dāsī⋅karete dhari’
du̐he nivedibe, du̐hāra mādhurī,
heriba nayana bhari’
en_translation:
Oh, my Gaura-kiśora! When, out of mercy, will You, the thief of my heart, appear in Śrī-godruma-vana?

Inside the joyful and pleasant grove, with Gadādhara on Your left, with a golden complexion and curly hair, dressed beautifully for dancing.

As I gaze, Śrī Rādhā-Mādhava will shine forth in a beautiful form, dancing with the sakhīs, a charming garland around Your neck.

Anaṅga-Mañjarī, being merciful, will hold this maidservant's hand and present the sweetness of both to me. I will behold them with my eyes completely filled.
